claim_status: No-claim toolchain-source manifest only; official toolchain documentation is not an independent fresh-host run, compiler/SDK/linker equivalence proof, owner-reviewed readiness, release confidence, or implementation evidence.

sources.title: Rustup toolchains
sources.observation: Rustup supports named channels, dated channels, fully versioned toolchains, and host target tuples; a channel name alone is not a stable compiler identity.
sources.run_record_consequence: Record the exact toolchain string, host target tuple, rustup version, resolved rustc --version --verbose output, and installation/channel provenance.

sources.title: Cargo build
sources.observation: Cargo --locked requires dependency versions to match the lockfile, while --offline restricts network use to locally available registry and Git state.
sources.run_record_consequence: Record the lockfile hash, locked/offline flags, registry and cache roots, network mode, and whether dependencies were fetched before an offline attempt; neither flag proves a clean host or identical native toolchain.

sources.title: Rustup overrides and toolchain files
sources.observation: Rustup resolves command-line, environment, directory, and rust-toolchain.toml overrides in a defined order; a checked-in toolchain file can pin a channel, components, targets, and profile, and Cargo.lock should normally be tracked with a pinned toolchain.
sources.run_record_consequence: Record the discovered rust-toolchain.toml path and contents hash, override source and precedence, active toolchain from rustup show, requested components/targets/profile, and lockfile identity; do not infer the active compiler from a machine default.

sources.title: Use the Microsoft C++ Build Tools from the command line
sources.observation: Microsoft distinguishes installed C++ workloads from the command-line environment that exposes compiler, linker, library, and related tools; the selected toolset and developer environment determine the executable paths used by a build.
sources.run_record_consequence: Capture the exact developer-shell or vcvars initialization command, environment delta, cl/link paths and versions, target architecture, LLVM/lld path if used, and shell type; a normal PowerShell PATH is not evidence that the intended compiler environment was active.

sources.title: Windows SDK overview
sources.observation: The Windows SDK supplies versioned platform headers, libraries, metadata, and build tools for Windows application targets; an installed SDK family is a distinct build input from the Visual Studio product or MSVC toolset.
sources.run_record_consequence: Record the selected Windows SDK version, installation root, headers/libraries/resource-tool paths, target architecture, and discovery mechanism, then retain the exact SDK facts with the linker and compiler records.

sources.title: Acquire Microsoft C++ Build Tools
sources.observation: MSVC Build Tools installations and toolsets can coexist, and serviced stable, older, and preview channels are distinct environment choices.
sources.run_record_consequence: Record the Visual Studio channel and installation instance, workload/component IDs, MSVC toolset, Windows SDK, linker path/version, and preview/stable status rather than recording only that Visual Studio is installed.

unsupported: No independent fresh-host or owner-approved clean-VM run has been executed.
No owner-reviewed PB-008 or PB-009 readiness exists.
No compiler, SDK, linker, cache, or host equivalence claim exists.
No release-confidence, production, Chrome-class, or implementation claim exists.
